_comment: Zenodo's GitHub integration reads this file at release time. When you tag a new release on GitHub (e.g. v0.6.0), Zenodo automatically creates a deposit with the metadata below. Edit version/upload_type/keywords here, not in the Zenodo UI, so the metadata stays version-controlled.
title: OpenEtruscan — open-source digital corpus platform for Etruscan epigraphy
description: OpenEtruscan is an open-source platform for Etruscan epigraphy. It unifies the inscriptions into one corpus of 6,633 records (the cleaned public dataset is a 6,567-row subset), publishes them as Linked Open Data, and ships a multilingual encoder (LaBSE + XLM-R with Etruscan-side LoRA) that embeds Etruscan and Latin in a shared vector space. Includes the frozen benchmark rosetta-eval-v1 with random, Levenshtein, and model baselines. See research/FINDINGS.md for the headline results and research/notes/reproduce-rosetta-eval-v1.md for replication.
notes: Companion dataset (the 6,567-row cleaned corpus CSV) is deposited separately on Zenodo and cross-linked via related_identifiers in that deposit's metadata. The corpus is derived from the Larth Dataset (Vico & Spanakis 2023) and the Corpus Inscriptionum Etruscarum (Vol. I extractions); see research/BIBLIOGRAPHY.md for the full provenance chain.
